response: New patient appointments are scheduled Monday through Friday and typically last about 30 minutes.

We recommend arriving 15–30 minutes early for check-in and paperwork, especially for new patients. If you’d like, email shelbygimd@gmail.com and my supervisor can confirm what to expect for your visit.

Yes — I can help with scheduling. Appointments are Monday through Friday during office hours. To schedule, we’ll need your full name, date of birth, phone number, and insurance information. Depending on your plan, a referral from your primary care doctor may be required.
Appointments are offered Monday through Friday. If you email your preferred days and times to shelbygimd@gmail.com, my supervisor will email you the available openings and next steps.
If you email your preferred days and times to shelbygimd@gmail.com, my supervisor will email you the available openings and next steps.
No problem—we’ll note that you do not want to schedule from that referral right now.
Okay—we can cancel for now. When you’re ready, call back and we’ll find the next available date.
Thank you for letting us know. Giving notice helps avoid late-cancel fees. We can cancel now and reschedule once you’re ready.
Understood—if benefits show out-of-network or a very high copay, it’s reasonable to pause and verify IPA/plan. Call us once your insurance is corrected and we’ll reschedule.
If you need to cancel a procedure, please call as soon as possible. Many facilities require notice to avoid late-cancel fees.
Our clinic is located at 1320 El Capitan Drive, Suite 110, Danville, CA 94526. Procedures are performed next door in Suite 100 at the Executive Surgery Center.

We’re located at 1320 El Capitan Drive in Danville. Office visits are in Suite 110, and procedures are next door in Suite 100 at the Executive Surgery Center. If you tell me whether you’re coming for a clinic visit or a procedure, I can confirm the correct suite.
Procedures are performed at the Executive Surgery Center at 1320 El Capitan Drive, Suite 100, Danville, CA 94526 (next door to the clinic in Suite 110).
Office visits and consults are in Suite 110. Procedures are next door in Suite 100 at the Executive Surgery Center.
Our office/consult visits are at 1320 El Capitan Drive, Suite 110, Danville.
Procedures are at Executive Surgery Center next door: 1320 El Capitan Drive, Suite 100, Danville.
The time listed is your arrival/check-in time. Your procedure starts shortly after check-in.
We can confirm whether you are scheduled for colonoscopy, endoscopy, or both. Call us with your name/DOB and we’ll verify your scheduled procedures.
The office may close briefly for internal meetings. If you reach voicemail/closure message, call back after reopening or leave a message.
Typical office hours are around 9 AM–4 PM on business days, but call ahead to confirm same-day cutoff times.
Correct—we are typically closed on Saturdays.
That’s understandable. We’re calling from Dr. Shelby’s office in Danville. You can hang up and call us back directly at the number on our website to confirm.
Same-day pickup cutoffs vary. Call us and we’ll confirm today’s cutoff so you don’t miss the window.
Sometimes procedures don’t show up in MyChart. Our office schedule is the source of truth—call us and we can confirm your date/time.
We are typically not open on Saturdays.

To schedule a procedure, we typically need your full name, date of birth, phone number, insurance information, and the doctor’s order or recommendation after consultation/provider review. If you email your question to shelbygimd@gmail.com, my supervisor will email you the exact next steps for your situation.
No problem—we can note that you prefer to schedule after the holidays. Call us when you’re ready and we’ll offer the next available slots.
If your cardiologist advised holding off, we can postpone and note a follow-up timeframe (e.g., 6 months). Please call us when cleared.
If your oncologist wants you to wait, disregard reminders and call us when you’re cleared to proceed.
If there’s a high chance you can’t complete prep safely, rescheduling early is better than a last-minute cancellation. Call us and we’ll move your slot.
Okay—we’ll note that you’re holding off for now. Call us when you’re ready to proceed.
No problem—call us and we’ll reschedule. We can also send a text reminder once it’s booked.
No problem—we can reschedule you to the next available procedure day. Call us with your preferred dates/times.
We can tentatively hold an alternate slot, but please decide as soon as possible so we can release the extra appointment and avoid prep timing issues.
Understood. Please follow your oncologist’s timing and call us when you are cleared to proceed; you can disregard reminders until then.
No problem—tell us your preferred date range and time window, and we’ll reschedule to the nearest available slot.
If you have the flu and can’t safely prep, we’ll reschedule to the next available procedure day.
If you feel unwell or have fever, rescheduling may be best. If you feel okay on antibiotics, you may still proceed—call us for case-specific guidance.
No problem—many patients prefer scheduling after holidays. We can book the next available January slot when you’re ready.
We can reschedule—tell us your preferred date range and time (morning/afternoon), and we’ll offer the closest available slots.
No problem—tell us your travel window and we’ll schedule on the next available date that fits your calendar.
If you have a significant infection or dental surgery that may affect prep/diet, rescheduling is often safest. Call us and we’ll adjust your date.
If you’re running late, please call us. Appointments can be canceled if significantly late without contact.
If you’re ill (flu/strep/fever) and can’t safely do the prep, it’s best to reschedule. Call us and we’ll move your appointment.
We often have early morning arrival times (e.g., 6:00–7:30 AM) depending on the day. Tell us what time window works and we’ll match availability.
Some January schedules may include Friday procedure days in addition to Monday/Wednesday. We can offer the earliest available dates when scheduling.
Around holidays, procedure weeks can be limited. We can offer the next available Monday/Wednesday (or special openings) based on the schedule.
Latest arrival times can be around early afternoon (often up to ~1:15 PM), depending on the schedule. We can confirm for your chosen date.
Procedures are typically not on Tuesdays (most often Monday/Wednesday). We can offer the closest available Monday/Wednesday slot.
Procedures are typically on Monday and Wednesday. Thursdays/Fridays are usually not procedure days unless the schedule explicitly opens them.
Some holiday weeks shift procedure days (e.g., Wednesday/Friday). We’ll confirm the available dates when scheduling.
Procedure days can vary month-to-month, but Monday/Wednesday is the typical pattern. We can offer the next available date in the current open schedule.
Friday procedures are rare but can occur if the schedule is opened. Call us with your preferred dates and we’ll confirm availability.
Procedures are typically scheduled on Mondays and Wednesdays. Some holiday weeks can shift, but Monday/Wednesday is the usual pattern.
Please add our office number to your contacts and make sure Do Not Disturb and call forwarding are off. Dr. Shelby calls from our office number.
Consultations are typically scheduled Monday–Wednesday (in person or by phone, depending on the case).
We can check for cancellations. If an earlier slot opens, we’ll offer it if there’s enough time to prepare and you’re available.
Many consults are brief (about 10–15 minutes), though complex issues can take longer.
If constipation meds stopped working, we typically schedule a brief follow-up (phone or office) to reassess and adjust treatment.
We can often reschedule missed phone consults the same day if there’s availability. Call us with your best callback number.
If a medication hasn’t been used in a long time or isn’t listed as a refill, Dr. Shelby may require a brief consult before re-prescribing.
If you need a physical exam for a protruding rectal issue, an in-person visit is best. We’ll offer the earliest available.
Late-day in-office slots can be limited. If you need later than the last in-office slot, a phone visit may be the best option.
If your oncologist advised waiting, you can disregard reminders for now and call us when your oncologist clears you to proceed.
No problem—please call us after the new year when you’re ready to schedule. We’ll offer the next available openings.
Understood—we can note that you prefer scheduling in January. Call us when you’re ready and we’ll offer the next available January slots.
Caller ID can sometimes display an out-of-state area code. If you’re unsure, you can call the office back directly to verify.
If your cardiologist advised delaying procedures due to a clot, we can defer and set a reminder to revisit in the timeframe they recommended.
If you have dialysis or fixed medical appointments, tell us your available days and we’ll schedule around them (phone visits can also be an option).
Dr. Shelby typically calls from the office number. Add it to your contacts so it doesn’t get blocked.
We received your referral and are calling to schedule a consultation. If now isn’t a good time, call back when you’re free and we’ll book it.
If today is fully booked, we can offer the next available slot. If symptoms are severe, consider urgent care/ER.
I can connect you with our scheduler for procedure dates, prep selection, and insurance verification.
We can note your interest for a cancellation slot, but you must have enough notice to safely complete prep (usually at least 24 hours).
Phone follow-ups can be done while you’re traveling, as long as you can answer the call. We’ll schedule a time window for Dr. Shelby to call you.
For new patients, we schedule a consultation first (in person or by phone). After that, we can schedule any needed procedures.
Our phone visits are regular phone calls—no Zoom link.
We received your referral. We can schedule a consultation (phone or in-office), then proceed to scheduling any procedures if needed.
If it’s been over ~3 years since your last visit, Dr. Shelby typically requires a brief consult first (often for insurance documentation) before scheduling a procedure.
We often schedule procedures month-to-month. If your due date is far out, we can note it and reach out when that month opens.
Hoarseness/cough after lying down can be reflux-related. Dr. Shelby may recommend stronger acid control and an endoscopy to evaluate worsening hernia/inflammation.
Endoscopy-only is usually quicker to schedule and does not require colonoscopy prep—just fasting rules.
Esophageal strictures sometimes require repeat dilation. Dr. Shelby may recommend repeat dilation and follow-up if symptoms recur.
Some strictures require repeat dilations over months. Dr. Shelby will recommend timing based on your symptoms and prior response.
If symptoms persist, Dr. Shelby may recommend repeating an endoscopy to evaluate for inflammation, ulcers, hernia, or infection.
Postpartum reflux can change. If symptoms are persistent, Dr. Shelby may recommend endoscopy and a tailored medication plan (avoiding long-term PPI if possible).
If insurance is changing soon, tell us the deadline and we’ll try to schedule within the current coverage window when possible.
If reflux is worsening despite meds, Dr. Shelby often recommends an upper endoscopy (EGD) to evaluate for hernia, inflammation, ulcers, or infection.
